_comment: Model registry / experiment log. Each entry records how a model was trained, its results, and findings. Weights live in weights/ (gitignored, large); this JSON (metadata + notes) is committed so past efforts are recoverable. 'active' = the model the Inference tab / backend runs by default. status: active | archived. Edit notes freely, or via the Models tab.

models.name: Keypoint R-CNN v0 (overfit demo)
models.notes: First end-to-end pass — validated the pipeline (export -> train -> infer). Overfits: trained and evaluated on the SAME 15 centered chips (0.18 px error), but found 0 trucks on a coarse full-scene sliding window. MISTAKE FOUND: all crops were pixel-centered, so the model learned 'echo at dead center' and had no translation tolerance. Also: MPS (Apple GPU) produced NaN losses -> forced CPU. Superseded by the jitter model.

models.name: Keypoint R-CNN v1 (+ translation jitter)
models.notes: FINDING (good): translation-jitter augmentation fixed the overfit-to-center problem. Coarse-stride sliding window recovered 3/3 trucks (vs 0/3 for v0), confidence jumped ~0.4 -> ~0.9. Still only 15 vehicles / 3 scenes, so it doesn't generalize across scenes. This is the model the backend used before the Centralia model.

models.name: Keypoint R-CNN (Centralia, small anchors)
models.notes: Trained on 3 Centralia scenes for the held-out visualization. Small anchors (8-128px, per Adamiak) for the tiny 4-8px streaks. FINDINGS: full-scene held-out F1 ~0.50 matches Van Etten 2024's PlanetScope truck F1 (0.49). False positives = bright road/lane-paint; misses concentrated in DENSE traffic (dedup merges neighbours) - a known failure in both papers. Currently the backend default. NOTE: trained on only 3 of the 4 Centralia scenes; a production model on all 339 is the next step.

models.name: Base (default anchors, no aug)
models.notes: BASELINE: default anchors (32-512px), NO augmentation, no data engineering. The zero-effort reference to measure every refinement against.
